docs.title: SlideMaker
docs.text: slidemaker is a Python library for generating slide decks from PowerPoint templates. Built on python-pptx, it exposes one high-level class (SlideBuilder) that handles template loading, slide cloning, placeholder replacement, content layout, style resolution, and final deck assembly.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
• src/slidemaker/core.py: low-level text, shape, layout, and slide utilities.
  • src/slidemaker/cli.py: SlideBuilder high-level API.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from slidemaker import SlideBuilder

sb = SlideBuilder("my_template.pptx")

# Replace {{title}} placeholder in template slide 1
sb.add_slide(
    template_page=1,
    content={"title": "Introduction", "subtitle": "Getting Started"},
)

# Create new shapes on default template page
sb.add_slide(
    content={"title": "Key Points"},
    items=["Point A", "Point B"],
)

sb.save("output.pptx")

See Getting Started for setup and examples.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
The canonical changelog is maintained in the repository root:

  • CHANGELOG.md

Releases are generated by semantic-release during CI release workflows.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/mediatoolbox-org/slidemaker.git
cd slidemaker
mamba env create --file conda/dev.yaml
conda activate slidemaker
poetry config virtualenvs.create false
poetry install --with dev

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
• keep changes focused
  • include tests for behavior changes
  • update docs when user-facing behavior changes
  • keep Python 3.10+ compatibility

docs.title: Release Model
docs.text: This repository uses semantic-release. Follow conventional commit style in PR titles/messages.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide sets up slidemaker for local development.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/mediatoolbox-org/slidemaker.git
cd slidemaker
mamba env create --file conda/dev.yaml
conda activate slidemaker
poetry config virtualenvs.create false
poetry install --with dev

docs.title: Prepare Your Template
docs.text: Add {{placeholder}} text to shapes in your PowerPoint template. For example, put {{title}} in the title textbox and {{body}} in the content area.

docs.title: Run Example
docs.text:
from slidemaker import SlideBuilder

sb = SlideBuilder("my_template.pptx", template_default_page=5)

# Replace placeholders in template slide 1
sb.add_slide(
    template_page=1,
    content={"title": "Introduction", "subtitle": "Course Overview"},
    notes="Welcome to the course.",
)

# Create new shapes on default template page
sb.add_slide(
    content={"title": "Key Concepts"},
    items=["Concept A", "Concept B"],
)

sb.save("test_deck.pptx")
